text.title: pnpm-catalogs Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for pnpm-catalogs. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: External dependencies that appear in two or more workspace packages MUST be declared once under `catalog:` (or a named catalog) in `pnpm-workspace.yaml` and referenced with `"<pkg>": "catalog:"` in every package.json. Internal workspace packages MUST be referenced as `"<pkg>": "workspace:*"`. Hard-coded versions (e.g., `"react": "^19.0.0"`) for shared deps are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Catalogs make version drift impossible by construction: there is exactly one place a version is defined. Without catalogs, agents adding the same dep in two packages will pick whatever range is current that day, producing duplicate trees and subtle compatibility breakages.

rule.statement: Use `--filter ...{path}` (with the leading dots) to operate on a package AND all of its dependents; `--filter {path}^...` for a package and its dependencies. CI uses `--filter ...[origin/main]` to test only what changed since main.
rule.rationale: Running `pnpm test` workspace-wide on every commit is unnecessary in monorepos with dozens of packages; filtered runs cut wall-clock time 5–20x while still exercising the affected slice. The leading-dots syntax is exact and standard; agents must use it instead of greps and shell glue.
